description: ARIA 2D Materials PSP Knowledge Graph (tiny example, 6 representative relationships)

causal_relationships.source: Re presence at the MoS2 growth front
causal_relationships.evidence_text: When Re is present at the growth front of a MoS2 grain, the energy of formation of edge sulfur vacancies is increased by ~1.4 eV, effectively suppressing vacancy formation.
This strongly suggests that the new phase we observe coincides with the predicted 2x2 CDW and that the CDW formation is driven by the extremely high levels of electron doping from Li intercalants.

When the vacancies are arranged with periodicity ns=2, localized defect modes occur at higher frequencies, essentially realizing a phonon band gap that reduces scattering between acoustic and optical phonons, enhancing MFP.
A ferromagnetic coupling is realized in the range phi_SO in (pi/3, 5pi/3) while an antiferromagnetic coupling is found in the interval phi_SO in (-pi/3, pi/3).
The 0.1 at.% Re-MoS2 BGFET exhibits a maximum IDS of ~61.5 uA/um, nearly 10x higher than pristine MoS2 (~7.6 uA/um), which is attributed to reduced defect-related trap states.

causal_relationships.target: magnetic exchange coupling parameters and Curie temperature
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Hole doping increases spin splitting energy, magnetic anisotropy energy, and magnetic exchange coupling parameters, resulting in enhanced Curie temperatures.
